talk_metadata.talk_title: Potentiating venetoclax response using the NAMPT inhibitor OT-82
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped OS data entirely, presented only PFS

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Thrombocytopenia as dose-limiting toxicity not fully addressed
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted DLT for OT-82 is thrombocytopenia

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that older NAMPT inhibitors had retinal toxicity, implying OT-82 may share class liabilities

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify biomarkers (e.g., KMT2A rearrangement, FLT3 mutation) that predict OT-82 + venetoclax response
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted higher response in KMT2A‑rearranged and FLT3‑mutated PDXs but lacked statistical significance
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to model metabolic dependencies and predict responders

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine OT-82 with agents that mitigate thrombocytopenia
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned need for clinical protocols that mitigate thrombocytopenia
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict synergistic combos that spare platelets via pathway analysis

unresolved_questions: What specific biomarkers can reliably predict response to OT-82 + venetoclax?
Can thrombocytopenia be mitigated without compromising efficacy?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Non‑canonical CAR T cell states in multiple myeloma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped detailed functional assays for persistor cells

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of biomarkers to predict persistor CAR T cell emergence
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker admitted that many patients relapse despite BCMA expression
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients show durable responses with persistor T cells

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all BCMA‑positive patients without stratifying by CAR T cell functional state
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial monitoring of CAR T cell exhaustion or persistor state

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted that persistor state arises from multiple early CAR T subsets, suggesting platform flexibility but also lack of specificity
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad use of same CAR construct without addressing exhaustion mechanisms

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed high AP‑1 expression in persistor cells (suggesting activation) but concluded that these cells have low exhaustion markers, contradicting typical exhaustion signatures

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of persistor cytotoxicity

crispro_opportunity.description: Target AP‑1 driven persistor program to sustain CAR T activity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted AP‑1 genes (JunB, FosB) high in persistor cells
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model transcription factor network to identify small‑molecule inhibitors of AP‑1 that could prevent persistor formation

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop assay for early persistor signature to select patients for combination therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted persistor cells arise from multiple early states and correlate with durable response
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to integrate single‑cell data and predict persistor emergence

unresolved_questions: What is the functional capacity of persistor CAR T cells in vivo?
Can modulation of AP‑1 improve durability of response?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Molecular glue recruiting lysine acetyltransferases to BCL6
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped discussion of off‑target effects of KatTCIP

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Reliance on BCL6 expression; low BCL6 tumors may not respond
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some high‑BCL6 lines responded dramatically, implying subset efficacy

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: KatTCIP platform linking BCL6 BTB inhibitor to P300/CBP bromodomain inhibitor
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed limited protein‑protein interface (only two neo‑hydrogen bonds), suggesting modest cooperativity
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Potential lack of potency in tumors with heterogeneous BCL6 expression

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed potent activation of BCL6‑dependent transcription (high GFP) but concluded that global acetylation is unchanged, which seems contradictory

key_data_gaps: No in vivo efficacy in solid tumor models
Lack of toxicity data beyond weight monitoring

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO to redesign KatTCIP linkers for stronger ternary complex formation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted limited plasticity of ligand binding and only two neo‑hydrogen bonds
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict optimal linker geometry to increase cooperativity

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine TCIP3 with MYC inhibitors to overcome residual MYC activity
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker reported residual MYC expression despite BCL6 activation
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model network to identify synergistic targets

unresolved_questions: Can TCIP3 be effective in low‑BCL6 expressing lymphomas?
What are the long‑term safety implications of recruiting P300/CBP globally?

talk_metadata.talk_title: FGFR1‑positive CAF inhibition to improve CAR‑T efficacy
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped detailed toxicity assessment of FGFR inhibitor

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential off‑target effects on normal fibroblasts not addressed
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Combination rescued CAR‑T refractory models, implying subset benefit

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all relapsed AML patients without selecting for FGFR1‑positive CAF signature
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small preclinical cohort, risk of over‑interpretation

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: FGFR1 inhibitor used to target CAFs
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the inhibitor is not CAF‑specific and may affect other FGFR1‑expressing cells
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Broad use without patient selection could dilute signal

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a gene‑expression signature for FGFR1‑positive CAFs to select patients for FGFRi + CAR‑T
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted that only mesenchymal microenvironment tumors responded
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO to integrate scRNA‑seq CAF data and predict responders

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine FGFRi with agents that boost macrophage recruitment (e.g., CSF1R agonists)
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: FGFR inhibition increased MCP chemokines and macrophage infiltration
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model cytokine network to identify synergistic combos

unresolved_questions: What is the safety profile of chronic FGFR1 inhibition in patients?
Can FGFR1 expression be reliably measured in routine biopsies?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Condensate dynamics drive adaptive METTL3 inhibitor resistance
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped detailed pharmacokinetic data for STC‑15

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: YTHDC1 condensate formation not addressed in original trial design
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted that overexpression of YTHDC1 caused resistance without clear mechanistic explanation
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Some tumors showed durable response when YTHDC1 was low

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No stratification by YTHDC1 expression or condensate phenotype
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial monitoring of condensate properties

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Resistance mediated by YTHDC1 condensate adaptation, a mechanism not covered by current IP
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Lack of combination strategy to prevent condensate‑driven resistance

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that METTL3 inhibition reduces MYC/BCL2 mRNA stability yet later claimed residual M6A and YTHDC1 condensates rescue MYC/BCL2 protein levels

key_data_gaps: No clinical outcome data from Phase 2
Lack of patient‑level YTHDC1 biomarker data

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop YTHDC1 inhibitor or degrader to prevent condensate‑mediated resistance
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated that YTHDC1 inhibition reverses resistance in vitro
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model condensate dynamics to predict optimal dosing schedules

crispro_opportunity.description: Use YTHDC1 expression/condensate liquid‑like score to select patients for METTL3 inhibitor therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted correlation between condensate properties and drug response
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate imaging data with CrisPRO to generate predictive biomarker

cited_competitors.name: Venetoclax
cited_competitors.context: Combined with METTL3 inhibitor to overcome resistance

unresolved_questions: What is the safety profile of combined YTHDC1 and METTL3 inhibition?
Can condensate imaging be standardized for clinical use?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Lymphotoxin‑α activation of TNFR1 induces AML cell death
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped discussion of potential cytokine storm risk

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Potential systemic inflammation and cytokine release not evaluated
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: No cytopenia observed in mice but human safety unknown

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No selection for TRAF2‑low AML patients

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed early‑stage modifications; IP may be narrow
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on cytokine delivery without clear dosing regimen

key_data_gaps: Human safety and cytokine release data missing

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop assay for TRAF2 deficiency to identify AML patients likely to respond
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed TRAF2 repression across AML subtypes correlates with sensitivity
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model TRAF2 network to predict responders

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine lymphotoxin‑α with agents that further down‑regulate TRAF2 or enhance necroptosis

unresolved_questions: What is the risk of systemic inflammation in patients?
Can TRAF2 levels be reliably measured in clinical samples?

talk_metadata.talk_title: MET12L as a novel player in platelet dysfunction in myeloid neoplasms
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we don't fully understand why some patients escaped
Skipped detailed discussion of potential bleeding risk

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Impact on megakaryocyte maturation not fully dissected
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: MET12L deletion reduces thrombocytosis in JAK2‑V617F mice

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: No therapeutic agent yet; target is loss‑of‑function
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Lack of druggable approach to modulate MET12L

crispro_opportunity.description: Explore MET12L as a therapeutic target to mitigate thrombocytosis in MPN patients
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker showed MET12L deletion counteracts thrombocytosis in JAK2‑V617F mice
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model mediator complex alterations to design small molecules that mimic MET12L loss

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop MET12L expression assay to identify patients with high platelet counts
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Single‑cell RNA‑seq data linked MET12L expression to platelet features in MPN/MDS patients
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate scRNA‑seq with CrisPRO to predict high‑MET12L responders

unresolved_questions: Does MET12L loss affect bleeding risk in patients?
